content.question: The durability of any structural reform depends on whether future governments will honor or reverse it; Hungary has not established mechanisms (sunset clauses, cross-party safeguards, or independent review triggers) that would constrain reversal as Poland did not.
content.reduction_path: Explicit cross-party negotiation and constitutional or legal anchoring of any selection-structure reform in a way that requires supermajority or multi-party consensus to reverse; post-reform monitoring framework with published annual equity/achievement reports binding on future governments.
